name: Wai Kai Splash Pad
tagline: West Oahu's waterfront splash zone with floating obstacle course — worth the drive for a full day.
bestTime: Weekday mornings for the least crowds; bring sunscreen for the outdoor areas
kamaaina: Kamaaina rates available with valid Hawaii ID; check website for current pricing
talkStory: Wai Kai opened in 2023 as part of a waterfront development in Ewa Beach built around a 2.5-acre artificial lagoon designed by engineers behind Typhoon Lagoon at Disney World. The wave pool generates surfable standing waves while the floating obstacle course is a hit with kids 6 and up. The splash pad features zero-depth entry and gentle water features for toddlers. Multiple restaurants on-site mean no packing food -- try Nalu for poke overlooking the lagoon.
